detection_signals: All quality Champagne must age for 10 years minimum
Cava aged for less than 10 years is not a quality product
Traditional method sparkling wine quality is determined by lees contact duration
why_incorrect: Traditional method wines have minimum lees aging requirements set by regulation (15 months for Champagne, 9 months for Cava), not 10 years. Many quality producers age well beyond minimums (2-5+ years).
misconception: Traditional method sparkling wine (Champagne, Cava) must age in the bottle on lees for a minimum of 10 years to be considered high quality.
corrected_understanding: Traditional method sparkling wines have regulatory minimum lees aging periods. Quality producers often exceed these minimums, but high-quality wines can be produced at minimum requirements.
